sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Baozun (BZUN) CFO receives 300,000 RSU grant with 2029–2030 vesting
feed.summary: Baozun Inc.'s CFO, Zhu Yanjie, has been granted 300,000 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) tied to Class A ordinary shares. These RSUs will vest in two tranches: 75% on January 1, 2029, and the remaining 25% on January 1, 2030, aligning the CFO's compensation with the company's long-term performance. Following this grant, Zhu Yanjie directly holds 433,353 shares-related interests.

feed.title: Barfresh outlines $28M-$32M fiscal 2026 revenue and $3.2M-$3.8M adjusted EBITDA, amid manufacturing ramp
feed.summary: Barfresh Food Group (BRFH) has provided a strong financial outlook for fiscal 2026, projecting revenues between $28 million and $32 million and adjusted EBITDA between $3.2 million and $3.8 million. This positive forecast comes as the company continues to ramp up its manufacturing capabilities. The optimistic guidance suggests Barfresh anticipates significant growth and improved profitability in the coming years.

feed.title: BRFH: Record revenue, new manufacturing, and major school contract set stage for rapid 2026 growth
feed.summary: Barfresh Food Group Inc. (BRFH) achieved record revenue in 2025 and acquired a transformative asset which resolved supply constraints, enabling in-house manufacturing. This strategic positioning is expected to drive aggressive growth in 2026, with revenue projected to nearly double and margin recovery anticipated as facility upgrades are completed.

feed.title: Barfresh Food Group (NASDAQ: BRFH) doubles down with 2026 growth and EBITDA targets
feed.summary: Barfresh Food Group (NASDAQ: BRFH) reported record revenue of $14.2 million in 2025, a 33% increase year-over-year, largely due to the Arps Dairy acquisition. Despite a significant drop in gross margin to 22% and a wider Adjusted EBITDA loss, the company is projecting ambitious 2026 revenue of $28–$32 million and positive Adjusted EBITDA of $3.2–$3.8 million. This growth is expected to be fueled by an integrated manufacturing facility and recent financing, though the facility's ramp-up extends to Q4 2026.

feed.title: BWX Technologies (BWXT) director receives new dividend equivalent rights
feed.summary: BWX Technologies director Daniel L. Jablonsky received an automatic grant of 0.160 dividend equivalent rights. These rights are tied to a previously awarded restricted stock unit grant and represent a contingent claim to receive one share of BWX Technologies common stock. The delivery of these rights is aligned with the underlying RSUs according to Jablonsky's deferral election, categorizing this as a non-cash, compensation-related acquisition rather than an open-market trade.

feed.title: Black Rifle Coffee Company (NASDAQ: BRCC) seeks 1-for-10–1-for-50 reverse split ahead of vote
feed.summary: Black Rifle Coffee Company (BRCC) is seeking shareholder approval for a reverse stock split of its Class A common stock at a ratio between 1-for-10 and 1-for-50, aiming to regain compliance with NYSE's minimum bid price requirement. The company's 2025 financial highlights include $398.3 million in revenue and significant distribution growth, although gross margins were affected by increased coffee costs. Shareholders will vote on this proposal, along with director elections and auditor ratification, at the 2026 virtual Annual Meeting on May 28, 2026.

feed.title: Traws Pharma (TRAW) delays 2025 Form 10-K, cites financing and going-concern review
feed.summary: Traws Pharma (TRAW) has announced a 15-day delay in filing its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company attributes this delay to recurring operating losses, ongoing efforts to secure financing to meet Nasdaq listing requirements, and the need for KPMG LLP to complete audit procedures following management's going-concern assessment. Despite the delay, Traws Pharma provided preliminary financial results for 2025, projecting $2.8 million in revenue and a net income of approximately $9.2 million.

feed.title: BP p.l.c. (LON:BP) Given Average Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Eight brokerages have given BP p.l.c. (LON:BP) a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy," with five recommending a buy and three a hold. The average one-year target price for the stock is GBX 493. BP's shares recently traded up 3.1% to GBX 602 on heavy volume, and insiders collectively own approximately 0.26% of the company.

feed.title: Robert Walsh retirement and consulting deal at Bogota Financial (NASDAQ: BSBK)
feed.summary: Bogota Financial Corp. announced that Robert Walsh, EVP and Chief Lending Officer of Bogota Savings Bank, will retire effective March 31, 2026. A Retirement and Consulting Agreement has been put in place, which includes a $15,000 lump sum payment, immediate vesting of stock options, and a consulting fee of $10,000 per month through July 2026. The agreement also contains non-solicitation and confidentiality clauses.

feed.title: BranchOut 2025 10-K: $13.7M Revenue, $(0.57) EPS
feed.summary: BranchOut reported a net revenue of $13.7 million for 2025, which is a 113% increase year-over-year, driven by higher sales volumes and new product introductions. Despite this growth, the company recorded a net loss per share of $(0.57) due to increased operating expenses during its scale-up phase. The company also improved its gross margin through a shift to in-house production at its Peru facility and expanded distribution with national retailers.

feed.title: World Kinect (NYSE: WKC) chair uses 8,927 shares for RSU tax
feed.summary: World Kinect's Executive Chairman, Michael J. Kasbar, had 22,684 restricted stock units (RSUs) vest on March 28, 2026. To cover the associated tax liability, the issuer withheld 8,927 common shares at a reference price of $23.36 per share. Following this transaction, Mr. Kasbar directly holds 1,085,110 common shares and indirectly holds 1,340 shares through his spouse.

feed.title: Boqii (NYSE: BQ) Co-CEO details multi-class share holdings

feed.title: Foster Karen A., chief quality and operations, sells BioLife Solutions (BLFS) shares for $49k

feed.title: Allbirds loses its wings: how the $6 billion footwear dream fell apart
feed.summary: Allbirds, once a celebrated sustainable footwear company valued at over US$4 billion, announced it is winding down, with its assets and intellectual property to be sold for US$39 million. This article chronicles the company's dramatic rise and fall from its 2014 Kickstarter origins to its recent struggles with declining revenue, lawsuits, and a plummeting stock price. The timeline details key milestones, product launches, controversies, and financial challenges that led to the company's eventual demise within a decade.

feed.title: Andrea Jung shifts Wayfair (NYSE: W) shares to trust and gains RSUs
feed.summary: Wayfair director Andrea Jung reported a reclassification of 15,703 Class A shares from her individual holdings to a revocable trust, of which she is the sole trustee and beneficiary. Additionally, she was granted 3,177 restricted stock units (RSUs) which will vest over time based on service conditions, starting November 1, 2025. These transactions primarily represent a change in the form of ownership and an equity grant rather than open-market sales.

feed.title: Jorey Chernett reports 14.1% Neuronetics (STIM) holding in 13D
feed.summary: Jorey Chernett has filed a Schedule 13D, disclosing a 14.12% stake in Neuronetics, Inc. (STIM), representing 9,778,718 shares acquired for $19,557,436 through private transactions. Chernett, a private investor, believes the stock was undervalued and holds sole voting and dispositive power over his investment. feed.title: Blue Foundry Bancorp 10-K: $95.3M Interest Income, $(0.51) EPS
feed.summary: Blue Foundry Bancorp reported total interest income of $95.3 million and a net interest income of $47.4 million for the year. This growth was driven by higher loan yields and an expansion of its loan book. Despite these revenue drivers, the company recorded a net loss of $(10.0) million, or $(0.51) per share, due to a higher provision and elevated non-interest expenses, including merger-related costs.

feed.title: Stellantis Reports Q3 2025 Estimated Consolidated Shipments of 1.3 Million Units, +13% y-o-y
feed.summary: Stellantis N.V. reported an estimated 1.3 million consolidated shipments for Q3 2025, marking a 13% year-over-year increase. This growth was primarily fueled by a strong rebound in North America, with a 35% increase, supported by gains in Enlarged Europe and Middle East & Africa. North America's performance was boosted by normalized inventory dynamics and initial deliveries of HEMI® V8-powered Ram 1500, while Enlarged Europe saw growth through new "Smart Car" platform models.

feed.title: CBL International (NASDAQ: BANL) sets April 28 AGM on board, auditor
feed.summary: CBL International Limited (NASDAQ: BANL) has scheduled its annual general meeting (AGM) for April 28, 2026, in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. Shareholders will vote on the election or re-election of three directors and the ratification of MRI Moores Rowland LLP as the independent auditor for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, with an audit fee of US$221,000. Only shareholders of record as of April 2, 2026, will be eligible to vote, with Class A shares carrying ten votes and Class B shares one vote.

feed.title: TD (TD) offers $1,000 auto‑callable, contingent‑coupon notes linked to AVGO/META/NVDA/TSLA
feed.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering $1,000 equity-linked, auto-callable notes with contingent coupons tied to the performance of Broadcom (AVGO), Meta (META), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Tesla (TSLA). These notes feature a minimum 24.85% annual contingent coupon rate, an April 10, 2026 pricing date, an April 15, 2026 issue date, and an April 13, 2029 maturity date. Payments and automatic calls are contingent on the lowest performing underlying stock, exposing investors to significant downside risk if any one of the stocks performs poorly.
